channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Coin-per-view: the Bilibili metric that beats subscriber count for creator vetting
item.description: If you've ever sponsored a YouTube creator and been disappointed by the ROI, you've already lived through what subscriber count actually measures: not engagement, not influence, not purchase...

item.title: Upgrading Kiwi-chan’s Brain: Pushing a 30GB "Frankenstein" GPU Rig to the Limit with Qwen 3.6-35B-A3B
item.description: Upgrading Kiwi-chan’s Brain: Pushing a 30GB "Frankenstein" GPU Rig to the Limit with Qwen 3.6-35B-A3B If you’ve been following my journey of building "Kiwi-chan" (my autonomous Minecraft AI), you...

item.title: Como restaurar chaves SSH
item.description: Aprenda o que é uma chave SSH, quais são os tipos (ed25519, RSA, ECDSA) e como configurar corretamente no Linux, macOS e Windows com segurança. O que é uma chave SSH? Uma chave SSH é um par de...

item.title: Atomic Redis Value Replacement Without Downtime: The Temporary Key Pattern
item.description: Atomic Redis Value Replacement Without Downtime: The Temporary Key Pattern When Redis holds mission‑critical data—authorization rules, feature flags, live configuration—native key updates can...

item.title: The easiest Chinese platform to scrape in Python in 2026: Bilibili in under 30 lines
item.description: If you've ever tried to scrape RedNote (Xiaohongshu), you know the pain. Request signing that rotates monthly, TLS fingerprinting that blocks requests immediately, residential proxies required,...

item.title: OpenTelemetry in .NET Microservices: A Practical Guide to Observable Distributed Systems
item.description: You've deployed your fifth microservice. Requests flow through an API gateway, hit an order service, fan out to inventory and payment, and eventually land in a notification pipeline. Everything...

item.title: Adding OAuth 2.1 to your MCP server in TypeScript
item.description: If you're building an MCP server, sooner or later someone is going to ask: how does authentication work? The MCP spec leaves this open. Most early servers shipped with no auth at all, or a...

item.title: The Real Story of AI Agents Isn’t Intelligence. It’s Trust.
item.description: Everyone is talking about what AI agents can do. Write code. Use tools. Automate workflows. Search systems. Analyze documents. Coordinate tasks. That part is exciting. But the real question isn’t...

item.title: Complete Cost Comparison: Email APIs for AI Agents in 2026
item.description: Your AI agent needs email. Not just "send a notification" email — a real inbox where it can receive messages, reply in threads, and operate autonomously. The choice of email API determines what...

item.title: Your Digital Self Will Outlive You. Your Resume Already Does.
item.description: Altered Carbon imagined consciousness stored in a stack. We are not there yet. Not really. You cannot pull a person out of one body, drop them into another, and keep the same mind running like...

item.title: Ubuntu’s AI plans have Linux users looking for a ‘kill switch’
item.description: Canonical's plan to add AI features to Ubuntu has some users asking for "a version of Ubuntu that does not include these features," while others say they'll stick with older versions of the Linux...

item.title: Sanctioned Chinese AI Firm SenseTime Releases Image Model Built for Speed
item.description: With US restrictions limiting its access to advanced tech, SenseTime is doubling down on open source with a new model optimized to run on Chinese-made chips.

item.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
Google Photos uses AI to make the iconic closet from ‘Clueless’ a reality
item.description: Google recreates Cher's closet from "Clueless" with AI.

item.title: More Gemini features are coming to Google TV
item.description: Google TV just got more Gemini features, including the ability to transform photos and videos with tools Nano Banana and Veo.

item.title: SAP-Related npm Packages Compromised in Credential-Stealing Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are sounding the alarm about a new supply chain attack campaign targeting SAP-related npm Packages with credential-stealing malware. According to reports from Aikido...

item.title: Motorola just revealed the Razr Fold’s price and hoo boy
item.description: 2026 is shaping up to be a tough year to launch a high-end phone. The memory crisis has phone prices rising across the board, so an already expensive phone risks becoming a much too expensive...

item.title: The new Razr Ultra is still the best-looking phone out there
item.description: Look, you're not going to find much new on the 2026 version of the Motorola Razr Ultra. There's a new main camera sensor, a slightly bigger battery, and a higher price: $1,499, up from $1,299. But...

item.title: The Moto Razr and Razr Plus are victims of shrinkflation
item.description: The memory crisis claims another couple of victims. Motorola's midtier and entry-level flip phones cost $100 more than their predecessors, and have few upgrades to show for it. The 2026 Razr Plus...

item.title: Google Photos launches an AI try-on feature for clothes you already have
item.description: Google Photos is launching a new AI-powered feature you can use to virtually try on clothes you already have. Using the photos in your gallery, Google will create a virtual "wardrobe," allowing...

item.title: Microsoft is giving its Xbox employees an Xbox email address
item.description: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma wants everyone to know: "We are Xbox." Just last week, she scrapped Microsoft Gaming as the name of Microsoft's gaming division in favor of simply Xbox. Now, to reinforce that...

item.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of...

item.title: The best deals we found during Amazon’s Gaming Week
item.description: Amazon’s annual Gaming Week is bringing discounts on video games, accessories, and PC components through May 4th. This event is smaller than Amazon’s other sales, but there are some genuinely good...

item.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
Uber adds hotels to its app in big travel swing
item.description: Open Uber's app and you'll see options to hail a car, order some food, or go grocery shopping. And starting today, you'll see something new: hotels. In a major expansion into the world of travel...

item.title: Google TV is getting a dedicated row for YouTube Shorts
item.description: Google is making it easier to jump into watching YouTube Shorts on its TV devices without having to open the dedicated YouTube app. The Google TV homepage will soon introduce a new "Short videos...

item.title: New Wave of DPRK Attacks Uses AI-Inserted npm Malware, Fake Firms, and RATs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered malicious code in an npm package after a malicious package as a dependency to the project by Anthropic's Claude Opus large language model (LLM). The...

item.title: Firestorm Labs raises $82M to take drone factories into the field
item.description: A defense startup just raised $82 million to put drone factories inside shipping containers and bring manufacturing to the front lines.

item.title: Meet Shapes, the app bringing humans and AI into the same group chats
item.description: Think Discord chats, but with AI characters in addition to humans.

item.title: The Download: storing nuclear waste and orchestrating agents
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. It’s time to make a plan for nuclear waste Today, nuclear...
